revision.text:
[[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[File:God of Daishogun.png|thumb|120px|Daishōgun]] [[File:Statue of Daishogun.jpg|thumb|120px|Seated Statue of Daishōgun
in front of the Hōtoku Hall at {{ill|Daishōgun Hachijinja Shrine|ja|大将軍八神社|1=Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Hachijinja Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|qq=}} (Kyoto)]] '''Daishōgun''' (大将軍, たいしょうぐん, だいしょうぐん) is one of the {{ill|Eight Guardian Deities|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Guardian Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}} in {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}, governing the auspicious and inauspicious aspects of directions. This deity is also known as the "Great Demon King" or "Daiō Ten'ō" (魔王天王), a great ogre deity. In {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}, the {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|qid=Q3080781|lt=Honji|1=Honji suijaku}} (spiritual essence) of Daishōgun is identified as {{ill|Takejizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takejizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In ancient China, the morning star was called "Keimei" (啓明), and the evening star "Chōkō" (長庚) or "Taihaku" (太白), both associated with military affairs as celestial deities. This concept was incorporated into Japanese Onmyōdō, where it became associated with the deity {{ill|Taihaku Shin|ja|太白神|1=Taibai|2=ja|3=太白|lt=Taihaku-no-Kami|4=ko|5=태백|6=vi|7=Thái Bạch|8=zh|9=太白|10=zh-yue|11=太白|comment=changed title based on wikidata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Shin|qid=Q1305475}} and {{ill|Konjin|en|Konjin|qid=Q6429460|lt=Konjin|1=Konjin}}, eventually evolving into Daishōgun. These deities are connected with {{ill|Venus|en|Venus|qid=Q313|lt=Venus|1=Venus}} and are characterized by their association with {{ill|metal|ja|五行説#金気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/metal|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q218652}} and cutting tools, making them fierce gods feared for their influence on calendars and directions. Daishōgun resides in one direction for three years, and activities in that direction are considered inauspicious, particularly earth-related activities. The directional taboo caused by Daishōgun's presence is referred to as the "Three-Year Block" (三年塞がり). However, during the deity's designated "Travel Days" (遊行日), the taboos are temporarily lifted. The yearly directions of Daishōgun correspond to the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|qid=Q1072961|lt=Chinese zodiac|1=Earthly Branches}} as follows: * Years of the Pig (亥), Rat (子), Ox (丑): West * Years of the Tiger (寅), Rabbit (卯), Dragon (辰): North * Years of the Snake (巳), Horse (午), Goat (未): East * Years of the Monkey (申), Rooster (酉), Dog (戌): South === Travel Days === * Spring Doyo (before {{ill|Lixia|en|Lixia|qid=Q1191007|lt=Lixia|1=Lixia}}): Days from {{ill|Wood Rat|en|Wood Rat|qid=Q1190890|lt=Yang Wood Rat|1=Yang Wood Rat}} to {{ill|Tsuchinoto Tatsumi|ja|戊辰|1=Yang Earth Dragon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto Tatsumi|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (travels east) * Summer Doyo (before {{ill|Liqiu|en|Liqiu|qid=Q1147421|lt=Risshu|1=Liqiu}}): Days from {{ill|Hinoshinoe|ja|1=Yang Fire Rat|2=ja|丙子|1=Yang Fire Rat|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoshinoe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kanosarumi|ja|1=Yang Metal Dragon|2=ja|庚辰|1=Yang Metal Dragon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanosarumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (travels south) * Autumn Doyo (before {{ill|Lidong|en|Lidong|qid=Q1191018|lt=Ritto|1=Lidong}}): Days from {{ill|Kanosarumi|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanosarumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kinoshinoe Tatsumi|ja|1=Yang Wood Dragon|2=ja|甲辰|1=Yang Wood Dragon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshinoe Tatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (travels west) * Winter Doyo (before {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|qid=Q1364685|lt=Risshun|1=Lichun}}): Days from {{ill|Mizunoto Tatsumi|ja|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|壬子|1=Yang Water Rat|2=ja|3=壬子|4=zh|5=壬子|6=ko|7=임자|lt_ko=임자|8=fr|9=Rat de eau|10=de|11=Wasser-Ratte|12=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Rat|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto Tatsumi|qq=}} to {{ill|Hinoshinoe Tatsumi|ja|1=Yang Fire Dragon|2=ja|丙辰|1=Yang Fire Dragon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoshinoe Tatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Fire Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (travels north) Daishōgun is said to be the son of {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and later identified with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. This association further links Daishōgun to fierce and martial characteristics. === Daishōgun Shrines in Kyoto === During the early Heian period, {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} established four shrines dedicated to Daishōgun at the cardinal directions of the {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}} to protect the city: * East: {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}} in Sakyo-ku * West: {{ill|Daishōgun Hachijinja Shrine|ja|大将軍八神社|1=Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Hachijinja Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|qq=}} in Kamigyo-ku * North: {{ill|Imamiya Shrine|ja|今宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imamiya Shrine|qq=}} (Sessha Ekijinsha) and {{ill|Daishōgun Shrine|ja|大将軍神社 (京都市北区)|1=Daishōgun Shrine (Kita-ku, Kyoto)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Shrine (Kita-ku, Kyoto)|qq=}} in Kita-ku * South: {{ill|Fujinomori Shrine|ja|藤森神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomori Shrine|qq=}} in Fushimi-ku Additionally, the {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588442}} (now {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}) also venerates Daishōgun. Kyoto retains a neighborhood named after Daishōgun. === Recent Example === In 2000, a sewer project in {{ill|Yamagata (city)|en|Yamagata (city)|qid=Q205526|lt=Yamagata|1=Yamagata (city)}} faced delays due to residents citing Daishōgun's presence as a reason for refusing construction. This event was widely reported, revealing the continued cultural influence of directional taboos in certain regions of Japan."Sewer project in Yamagata delayed by 'curses' of Daishōgun," {{ill|Yomiuri Shimbun|en|Yomiuri Shimbun|qid=Q645218|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, April 8, 2000. == See Also == * {{ill|Directional Deity|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|1=Directional deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}} * {{ill|Daishōgun Shrine|ja|大将軍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Konjin|en|Konjin|qid=Q6429460|lt=Konjin|1=Konjin}} / {{ill|Taihaku|ja|太白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku|1=Taibai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1305475}} == References == (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] }} [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] [[ja:大将軍 (方位神)]][[zh:大將軍 (方位神)]] {{translated page|ja|大将軍 (方位神)|version=105161093|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q167008}} [[Category:Divination]] [[Category:Onmyōdō_deities]] [[Category:Venusian_deities]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
